set_fields.description: Ralph loop configuration needs a safe service boundary before adding UI. The service should resolve which loop is being configured and mutate every user-configurable field in canonical Ralph loop state without relying on Pi ambient session state.
set_fields.design: Add a Ralph configuration service/API that resolves a target from an explicit task id or the current Ralph-owned session, loads the canonical .pi/loops/state entry, validates lifecycle safety, and writes contract updates atomically. Allow edits for draft/paused/completed loops and for active loops only when no child iteration session is active; if a child is active, fail with a clear message telling the user to pause/finish the iteration first. Support updates for pacing fields, reflection fields, tools, agents, concrete execution profile recapture, and sandbox recapture. Keep system-managed Ralph handshake tools non-removable by representing them separately or enforcing them during validation.
set_fields.acceptance_criteria: A service-level API can resolve explicit and current-session Ralph loops, refuses ambiguous/missing targets with clear errors, exposes validated mutations for every field classified configurable by tau-o6hb, applies validated contract updates to .pi/loops/state/<task-id>.json, refuses unsafe active-child edits, prevents removal of required Ralph loop control tools, preserves system/runtime-only fields, and has tests for target resolution, lifecycle safety, and coverage of scalar settings plus tools, agents, and concrete execution recapture.
